result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, and the project builds cleanly with the behavior verified.

- **`EscapeField`** returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** parses fields left to right: a field starting with `"` consumes content until an un-doubled quote (doubled quotes become one literal `"`, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise it reads up to the next comma or end of line. A trailing comma produces a final empty field, and the empty line parses to one empty field.

`NeedsQuoting` is untouched. I verified with a throwaway console project (deleted afterward, nothing added to the fixture): the round-trip `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` reproduces every non-empty case exactly — including embedded commas, quotes, CR/LF, trailing empties, and quote-only fields — plus the empty-list and empty-line edge cases from the spec. All checks passed, and `dotnet build` finishes with 0 warnings and 0 errors (used 1 of the 10 build cycles).
